prophet/prophet-bounty-runner.reason: Market-creation bounty runner, not a trading skill. The runtime drives the Privy email-OTP flow, calls Prophet's `createMarket` GraphQL mutation, and persists earnings telemetry; it never places trade orders, never holds inventory, never runs an exit path, and never touches a CLOB or DEX. The pattern-based detector hits because the runtime mentions `monitor` (bounty pool monitor), `dry_run` (cron dry-run), `deposit` (deposit-recommendation step), and `positions` (commentary). Sibling Prophet skills (`prophet-adversarial-auditor`, `prophet-growth-agent`, `prophet-market-seeder`) are not flagged because they have less surface text; this one only flips when a follow-up PR's diff is rich enough to be picked up by the changed-skills walker (PR #467's larger diff was masked by a depth-1 fetch on CI, hiding the same false positive).

prophet/prophet-polymarket-edge.reason: Read-only at v1 by design (SKILL.md launch contract; design doc §13.10 / §15). `--yes-live` always exits non-zero. The runtime never places orders, holds inventory, or interacts with the CLOB. `scripts/trading_safety.py` exists ONLY to encode the three preconditions any future trading-enable PR must satisfy — its docstrings mirror trading-domain language from sibling Polymarket skills, which causes the pattern-based detector to classify this skill as trading. The order-book / marketability / pre-trade checklist rules apply only when execution is wired; at v1 they have nothing to validate. Issue #454 tracks the gate contract; revisit waivers when a future PR re-enables execution.
